channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Opinion: Supreme Court rulings on immigration, gun rights, and liability spark sharp criticism of its conservative majority
item.description: This opinion article argues that a series of recent U.S. Supreme Court decisions represent a major shift in American law and values under President Donald Trump's administration. The author focuses pr...

item.title: Irène Tunc: French Actress Known for Roles in 1950s and 1960s European Films
item.description: Irène Tunc was a French actress and model born in 1935 who became a notable figure in postwar European cinema during the 1950s and 1960s. With her dark hair, expressive eyes, and sophisticated yet sen...

item.title: CNN panel erupts in heated exchange over immigration policy and TPS for Haitians
item.description: A CNN panel discussion on immigration policy escalated into a heated shouting match between political commentator Keith Boykin and former Trump White House staffer Caroline Sunshine. The debate center...

item.title: Dog reacts jealously while watching another dog eat a hot dog at Miami Marlins game
item.description: This short CBC “The Moment” video highlights a light-hearted incident from a Miami Marlins baseball game that quickly caught public attention. Peter Silveira shares the story of his dog Jonah, who bec...

item.title: Dan Rather criticizes proposed SAVE Act over potential impact on women voters
item.description: Veteran journalist Dan Rather has expressed concern over a proposed voting legislation known as the SAVE Act, which he says could negatively affect women’s ability to register to vote. In an opinion p...

item.title: Tilman Fertitta's planned America 250 yacht visit to Venice sparks local protests ahead of historic festival
item.description: Tilman Fertitta, the billionaire businessman, owner of the Houston Rockets, and U.S. ambassador to Italy appointed by President Donald Trump, has drawn criticism from residents and activists in Venice...

item.title: Why API-level authorization, not client tooling, is the real security boundary in MCP server design
item.description: This article explores the architecture and security model behind an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server used as a team knowledge base system called intent-brain. The system sits on top of a Fastify HT...

item.title: Rep. Rich McCormick Faces Backlash After Describing Medicaid as 'Free Money' at Town Hall
item.description: Republican Rep. Rich McCormick of Georgia drew widespread criticism after comments he made about Medicaid during a town hall event. McCormick argued that providing what he described as 'free money' th...

item.title: 1843 Portrait Documents John Owen, One of the Earliest-Born People Ever Photographed
item.description: The article highlights the remarkable historical significance of an 1843 portrait of John Owen, an American centenarian born on April 16, 1735, in Salisbury, Connecticut. Owen lived to the age of 107,...

item.title: White House insider criticizes Ted Cruz over Iran deal remarks amid GOP internal tensions
item.description: A reported internal dispute within Republican circles has surfaced after a White House insider sharply criticized Senator Ted Cruz of Texas in response to his comments on a Trump-era foreign policy ag...

item.title: Federal Judge Orders Elon Musk to Testify in Lawsuit Over America PAC Voter Data Collection
item.description: A federal magistrate judge in Austin, Texas, has ordered Elon Musk to sit for a deposition as part of a fraud lawsuit involving his political action committee, America PAC. The case was filed by two A...

item.title: June Lockhart's Transition to Television Stardom in the 1950s
item.description: June Lockhart, born on June 25, 1925, and who passed away on October 23, 2025, at the age of 100, was a versatile American actress whose career spanned films, theater, and television. By the 1950s, sh...

item.title: Discovered Family Photo Album Documents Everyday Life During the Roaring Twenties
item.description: The article showcases a recently discovered family photo album that offers a personal look into everyday life during the 1920s. Rather than focusing on major historical events, the collection highligh...

item.title: Audio recording raises ethics and legal questions over RFK Jr.'s contacts with Iowa congressional candidates
item.description: A newly disclosed audio recording has raised questions about whether Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. improperly used his government position while speaking with political candidates during the ...

item.title: China's Low-Cost START Mission Joins International Efforts to Study Asteroid Apophis During Its 2029 Earth Flyby
item.description: The article examines the growing number of missions designed to study asteroid 99942 Apophis during its exceptionally close approach to Earth on April 13, 2029, when it will pass within approximately ...

item.title: SADC Finance Ministers to Convene in Harare to Strengthen Regional Financial Integration
item.description: The Southern African Development Community (SADC) is set to hold a high-level meeting of its Committee of Ministers of Finance and Investment, along with the Peer Review Panel, on July 2, 2026, in Har...

item.title: Markwayne Mullin Loses Temper in House Hearing, Exchanges Sharp Words with Rep. Rosa DeLauro
item.description: During a House Appropriations Homeland Security Subcommittee hearing on June 25, 2026, U.S. Secretary of Homeland Security Markwayne Mullin became involved in a heated confrontation with Rep. Rosa DeL...

item.title: A Look at Carly Simon’s Early Life Through Childhood Photos and Family Background
item.description: The article presents a retrospective look at the childhood of singer-songwriter Carly Simon, using a collection of photographs from the 1940s and 1950s to illustrate her early life. Born in 1943 in Ne...

item.title: Legal Expert Dahlia Lithwick Criticizes Supreme Court Majority for Apparent Bias Toward Trump Administration in Recent Rulings
item.description: On June 25, 2026, Dahlia Lithwick, a prominent lawyer and journalist, appeared on MSNBC's 'Deadline: White House' and described some liberal Supreme Court justices as being 'incandescently angry' over...

item.title: House Oversight Chair James Comer Dismisses Trump's Reflecting Pool Vandalism Claims as Low Priority
item.description: House Oversight Committee Chairman James Comer (R-KY) stated that his panel has no plans to investigate the alleged vandalism of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, a topic President Donald Trump ha...
